name: Taiwan Rou Gui Oolong
terroir: Varies by growing area; distinct from the mineral-rich Wuyi Mountain terroir that defines mainland Rou Gui
flavor_profile: The Rou Gui cultivar grown in Taiwan and processed in the Taiwanese style — lighter roast and lower oxidation than its Wuyi counterpart. Retains the cultivar's signature cinnamon spice but wrapped in a cleaner, more floral package. Less mineral depth than Wuyi Rou Gui, more bright sweetness and a smoother finish.

brewing.rinse_hint: A brief rinse helps open the ball-rolled leaf and takes the edge off any roast char.
brewing.tips: Slightly hotter than unroasted gao shan to bring out the cinnamon character. Not as forgiving of boiling water as Wuyi Rou Gui — keep it under 95°C.
